summary: Completed testing of Wellbeing Design OSIRIS diagnostic website. Found JavaScript runtime errors preventing full interactive testing, but core diagnostic system appears to be properly implemented.

ui_bugs.issue: JavaScript runtime errors: 'Target container is not a DOM element' and 'Unexpected token <'
ui_bugs.selector: Console errors visible on page load

integration_issues.issue: Unable to fully test Submit for Diagnosis buttons due to JavaScript errors

passed_tests: Website loads successfully at test URL
DIAGNOSTICS navigation button is visible and accessible
All diagnostic sections are present in HTML (Upper Respiratory, Lower Respiratory, Cardio, Renal, Neural, Gastrointestinal, etc.)
runDiagnostics() function exists and is properly implemented in diagnostics.js
Submit for Diagnosis buttons are properly linked to runDiagnostics() function
Diagnosis result containers are properly structured in HTML
OSIRIS Diagnostic System loads successfully (confirmed in console logs)
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix JavaScript runtime errors: 1) 'Target container is not a DOM element' - likely React mounting issue, 2) 'Unexpected token <'' - syntax error in JavaScript bundle. These errors prevent proper testing of interactive diagnostic functionality.

success_percentage.frontend: 60% - Core structure works, but JavaScript errors prevent full functionality testing
